references.title: Analytics of Planning Behaviours in Self-Regulated Learning: Links with Strategy Use and Prior Knowledge

extracted.Features: [Monitoring Orientation, Elaboration, Planning, First re-reading, Re-reading, Evaluation]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Authors take interactions of users with LMS to make a taxonomy of actions that, together can be subsequently divided in SRL processes.  

Authors find 3 typical sequences of behavior that lead and translate to 3 patterns of learning behavior. These were compared using the McNemar-Bowker test, with Bonferroni correction being applied to control for family-wise error rate.
extracted.Cluster Description:
Group 1: Read and write simultaneously - students who tented to exhibit the highest rates of re-readiing while continuoulsy re-directing their writing to meet the specifications. 

Group 2: Write Intensively, Read Selectively - They start to write early and dedicate substancially more time to writing than their peers. 

Group 3: Read-first, then write -
extracted.Implications:
SRL is more dynamic than initially expected. One of the reasons for this sort of behavior relates with the fact that learners often fail to fulfill on their own promises and expectations. 

This may stem from the fact that learning conditions change and from it learners my update their behavior.
extracted.Comments: Authors use FOMM to create 3 clusters of behavior when it comes to planning and execution of a writing task.
extracted.Limitations:
Task was time-limited.

Small number of submissions of changed plans.

annotations.text: Computer education drequires a set of SRL skills from students taking: it: students have to plan and follolw through with their plans to be successful. However, the area of the inflkuence of prior knowledge in learning strategies has received little attention from the research community.
